Current enforcement and reporting surface for public-export tag requirements such as @category, @example, and @since.

Constructor contracts differ from regular functions.
Docgen's type export kind normalizes here.
Raw value declarations normalize away from docgen's const-specific term.
Constant declarations share the normalized value bridge kind.
Property tags stay distinguishable from general values.
Accessor-level tags need distinct rule targeting when present.
Enum documentation stays separate from value docs.
Module-level applicability maps to docgen fileoverview docs.
File-level comments share the fileoverview normalization target.
Explicit export specifiers map to re-export documentation.
Parameter structure is AST-derivable, but human-authored intent text is still required for high-quality documentation.
Return types are AST-derivable, but semantic return descriptions are not deterministic from syntax alone.
Throws metadata is only partially derivable in this repo because Effect error channels and conventional throws require semantic interpretation.
Version provenance is policy-driven and not available from the AST alone.
Category choices are documentation information architecture rather than code structure.
Examples require authored or generated code and are validated through docgen execution rather than AST inspection.
End-to-end bun run docgen execution path used for example compilation and generation.
Proposed future glue layer for semantic and procedural rule enforcement outside TrustGraph.
Implemented in current repo tooling without new ontology glue.
Modelable now, but enforcement requires external rule execution or semantic glue code.
Not a realistic target for the current TrustGraph ontology feature alone.
Symbol-level documentation block for a public API member.
Module or file-level documentation overview block.
Machine-readable docgen analysis record produced by repo CLI tooling.
Example code fragment validated through bun run docgen.
Require one @param line per declared parameter
Repo policy requires one @param line per declared parameter, but current enforcement needs AST-aware glue.
Require @returns for documented functions and methods
Repo policy requires @returns for every documented function contract, including void and assertion signatures, but current repo tooling does not enforce it.
Only use @throws when a function can actually throw
Determining whether thrown values are real runtime throws requires semantic or control-flow glue beyond the current ontology feature.
Do not use @throws for Effect error channels
This is a repo-specific semantic prohibition tied to Effect<A, E, R> interpretation and needs external rule execution.
Current docgen tooling already checks for example presence across public documentation surfaces.
Examples must compile via bun run docgen
The repo's zero-tolerance policy explicitly treats successful docgen example compilation as mandatory.
Current checker and analysis flows already report missing @since in public documentation paths.
Current docgen analysis treats @category as a required public-export tag, while checker enforcement lives in the higher-level analysis path.
Require description text on documented entries
Docgen runtime already reports missing descriptions for documented entries.
